backend/core/knowledge/root_cause_registry_v1.py:28-90 — 41 RootCauseTargetSpec entries confirmed. signal_vitamin_d_low registered at line 89: RootCauseTargetSpec('signal_vitamin_d_low', lrc.load_vitamin_d_low_hypotheses_v1, 'vitamin_d_low_hypotheses_v1.yaml'). RootCauseTargetSpec fields: signal_id, loader, asset_filename, registration_source='manual_v1'. NO activation_key field, NO source_spec_id field. Registry is signal_id-keyed only.

backend/core/analytics/root_cause_compiler_v1.py:36 — '_ROOT_CAUSE_TARGETS = get_root_cause_targets()' executes at import time with load_assets=True (root_cause_registry_v1.py:132). This validates ALL 41 existing hypothesis YAML entries via each loader at module startup. Any modification to ROOT_CAUSE_TARGET_SPECS that references a missing or invalid YAML will crash the pipeline at import. Shadow/pilot registry MUST be a completely separate module — see HARDENING_FLAG_shadow_registry_must_be_separate_module.

backend/core/analytics/root_cause_compiler_v1.py:424 — 'target = next((r for r in rows if str(r.get("signal_id", "")).strip() == target_signal_id), None)'. First-match on signal_id only; no activation_key lookup. After ARCH-RT-2, multiple rows may share the same signal_id with different activation_key. For signal_vitamin_d_low (single-frame, unique signal_id), this is safe. Contract doc must explicitly address multi-frame case per ARCH-RT-2 carry-forward requirement.

backend/core/knowledge/load_root_cause_hypotheses.py:33 — "if payload.get('schema_version') != 'v1': raise ValueError(...)" — validates string 'v1', not semver. New compiled hypothesis schema uses semver '1.0.0' (consistent with health_system_card_evidence_schema_v1.yaml). Compiled artefact loader must NOT use _load_hypotheses_asset(); it must be a separate loader validating against the compiled_hypothesis_schema_v1.yaml semver format. Existing YAML files in knowledge_bus/root_cause/hypotheses/ remain on 'v1' schema and are untouched.

knowledge_bus/root_cause/hypotheses/ — 40 files confirmed present including vitamin_d_low_hypotheses_v1.yaml and hba1c_hypotheses_v1.yaml. vitamin_d_low: schema_version='v1', primary_signal_id='signal_vitamin_d_low', 1 hypothesis ('vitamin_d_nutritional_status_context_v1'), confirmatory_tests=[] (empty — no test IDs to map). changes.citation: knowledge_bus/research/investigation_specs/inv_vitamin_d_low_deficiency_v1.yaml — spec_id: inv_vitamin_d_low_deficiency, signal_id: signal_vitamin_d_low. NO 'hypotheses' block present. Hypothesis-relevant content is in: evidence.physiological_claim ('Vitamin D is converted to its active form...'), evidence.evidence_strength (strong), narrative.mechanism, narrative.biological_pathway, supporting_markers (calcium: severity_marker, alp: mechanism_marker). The compiled hypothesis artefact must be authored as a manual translation from spec narrative/evidence content — not pipeline extraction from a formal hypotheses block. See HARDENING_FLAG_compile_is_manual_translation.

changes.result: CONFIRMED — compiled schema is richer than runtime DTO
changes.citation: backend/core/contracts/root_cause_v1.py:34 — RootCauseHypothesisV1 fields: hypothesis_id, title, summary (max 200), hypothesis_confidence, evidence_for (List[RootCauseEvidenceItemV1]), evidence_against, missing_data, confirmatory_tests, safety_class. Fields in sprint schema spec (physiological_claim, evidence_strength, evidence_for/against as text strings, contradiction_markers, missing_data_policy) are RICHER than current runtime DTO. Compiled artefact is the governance authority; runtime DTO is the emitted view. No change to RootCauseHypothesisV1 contract is required for the pilot — compiled loader must map from compiled schema to existing runtime DTO fields.

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:375-388 — 10 test categories: schema validation, artefact validation, loader success, loader fail-closed, confirmatory test mapping, divergence logic if coded, pilot shadow loading path, existing YAML path remains available, no full estate switch, multi-frame ambiguity handled or blocked. Compiled hypothesis loader emits the same RootCauseFindingV1 structure as the existing path.

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:390-408 — 15 STOP conditions covering: missing authority files (#1), no safe pilot (#2), schema cannot represent WHY content (#3), artefact validation failure (#4), confirmatory test mapping failure (#5), unresolved divergence (#6), silent multi-frame selection (#7), broad compiler changes (#8), package/spec modification (#9), SignalRegistry/SignalEvaluator modification (#10), PSI modification (#11), card evidence modification (#12), scope beyond one pilot (#13), compile manifest carry-forward violation (#14), tests cannot prove legacy YAML remains available (#15).

backend/core/analytics/root_cause_compiler_v1.py:36 — _ROOT_CAUSE_TARGETS = get_root_cause_targets() runs at import time with load_assets=True. Adding any new RootCauseTargetSpec to ROOT_CAUSE_TARGET_SPECS in root_cause_registry_v1.py before the compiled hypothesis YAML exists will crash the pipeline at import. The shadow/pilot mechanism MUST be implemented as a completely separate module (e.g., backend/core/knowledge/compiled_hypothesis_registry_v1.py) with its own loader. Do NOT modify ROOT_CAUSE_TARGET_SPECS or root_cause_registry_v1.py to add the pilot entry. The pilot shadow path must be callable independently, not via get_root_cause_targets().

knowledge_bus/research/investigation_specs/inv_vitamin_d_low_deficiency_v1.yaml — no 'hypotheses' block present. Hypothesis content is in evidence.physiological_claim, evidence.evidence_strength, narrative.mechanism, narrative.biological_pathway, supporting_markers. The compiled hypothesis artefact is authored by translating this content into the new compiled schema structure. The compiled_hypothesis_contract.md must explicitly state: (1) investigation specs do not currently contain a formal 'hypotheses' block; (2) the pilot artefact is manually authored from spec narrative/evidence content; (3) a future activation compiler would automate this translation; (4) the compile is NOT a runtime extraction from the raw spec. This prevents STOP #3 from being reargued during implementation.

backend/core/knowledge/load_root_cause_hypotheses.py:33 — validates schema_version == 'v1' (string). New compiled hypothesis schema must use semver string (e.g., '1.0.0') to be unambiguously distinct from legacy YAML schema_version: 'v1'. The compiled hypothesis loader must NOT call _load_hypotheses_asset() from load_root_cause_hypotheses.py, which enforces 'v1'. If any code accidentally passes a compiled artefact to the legacy loader (or vice versa), it will fail loudly — this is the correct fail-closed behaviour. Tests must confirm this cross-loading failure.

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:349-371 — out-of-scope list explicitly prohibits: migrating full root-cause estate; deleting/rewriting existing root-cause YAML; modifying PSI artefacts, package files, investigation specs, SignalRegistry, SignalEvaluator, Health Systems Card evidence, frontend, IDL copy, biomarker SSOT, scoring logic, unit conversion, clinical thresholds; performing full compile-manifest production locking; creating activation compiler; wiring PSI into runtime; introducing fallback parsers; committing helper scripts.

New compiled_hypothesis_schema_v1.yaml is a distinct authority from legacy YAML schema_version: 'v1'. Shadow/pilot registry is additive alongside existing ROOT_CAUSE_TARGET_SPECS — no duplicate authority source for existing signals. Existing YAML files remain the authority for all 41 registered signals during the pilot. Compiled artefact for signal_vitamin_d_low is a SHADOW/PILOT only — not replacing the existing YAML authority until estate migration is authorised.
